---
title: "grep & awk cheat sheet"
format: html
editor: visual
---py
---

# Grep Cheat Sheet

```         
grep [options] pattern [files]
```

<https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/grep-command-in-unixlinux/>

-   \^ link to full options table & code examples

| Option          | Decription                                   |
|-----------------|----------------------------------------------|
| -c              | just prints count                            |
| -i              | ignores case                                 |
| -n              | displays line numbers along with lines       |
| -v              | prints everything excluding pattern          |
| -A n            | also includes n \# of lines after            |
| -B n            | also includes n \# of lines before           |
| -C n            | includes n \# of lines both before and after |
| -f \[filename\] | takes patterns from a file line by line      |

# Awk Cheat Sheet

<https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/awk-command-unixlinux-examples/>

awk -F . '{print \$1, \$5}' completed_runs.txt

grep ".tbi" completed_runs.txt \| awk -F . '{print \$1}'

```         
grep -f file.txt file2.txt
```

### 

## Using both

-   getting info out of .gtf

```{bash}
cat pdestructans.gtf | grep "^NW" | awk '{print $1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $10, $12, $14, $16}' | awk '{gsub(/""/,"NA",$7); print}' | awk '{gsub(/;/,""); print}' | awk '{gsub(/"/,""); print}' | awk '{gsub(/Gene/,"NA",$9); print}' > pd_gtf-info.txt


cat pd_gtf-info.txt | awk '{print $1, $3, $4, $5, $6}' | grep "gene" > pd_gene_info.txt
# want to print out the gene rows

cat pd_gene_ref.bed | awk -v OFS='\t' '{print $1, $2, $3}' > pd_gene_ref.bed



cat pd_gtf-info.txt | awk -v OFS='\t' '{print $1, $3, $4, $5, $6}' | grep "exon" > pd_exon_info.txt

cat pd_exon_info.txt | awk -v OFS='\t' '{print $1, $3, $4}' > pd_exon_ref.bed

cat pdestructans.gff | grep "^NW" | awk '{print $3}' | uniq
```
